name: Alyz
description: The Alyz method is an intermediate Clock solving technique. It structures the solve into three front steps, a flip, three back steps, and four simultaneous corner moves. This balanced approach offers more optimization than beginner flip methods while being more accessible than the expert-level 7-Simul technique.

steps.name: Front Step 1
steps.description: Solve the first set of clocks on the front face using specific pin configurations and wheel turns. This begins the structured front-face solution.
tips: Plan front steps during inspection
Use efficient pin configurations to affect multiple clocks
Develop muscle memory for common front patterns

steps.name: Front Step 2
steps.description: Continue solving the front face by correcting additional clocks. Build on the progress from step 1 without disturbing already-solved positions.
tips: Maintain awareness of which clocks are solved
Choose pin configurations that preserve previous work
Flow smoothly from step 1 into step 2

steps.name: Front Step 3
steps.description: Complete the front-face work by solving the remaining front clocks. After this step, the front face should be in a favorable state for the flip.
tips: Ensure the front face is set up correctly before flipping
This step may partially solve back-face clocks as a bonus
Speed through this step with practiced sequences

steps.name: Flip
steps.description: Flip the Clock puzzle over to access the back face. The front face work is complete at this point.
tips: Flip quickly and consistently
Maintain your grip for a smooth transition to back-face solving
Mentally prepare for the back steps during the flip

steps.name: Back Step 1
steps.description: Begin solving the back face by correcting clocks using appropriate pin and wheel combinations.
tips: Be mindful of how back moves affect the solved front face
Use pin configurations that isolate back-face clocks
Plan back steps based on the state you see after flipping

steps.name: Back Step 2
steps.description: Continue solving the back face, building on the progress from back step 1.
tips: Maintain awareness of front-face preservation
Flow smoothly between back steps for speed
Recognize common back-face patterns for faster solving

steps.name: Back Step 3
steps.description: Complete the back-face edge clocks, setting up for the final corner moves.
tips: Ensure all edge clocks are correct before moving to corners
This step determines how easy the corner finish will be
Accuracy here prevents re-solving later

steps.name: 4 Simultaneous Corner Moves
steps.description: Solve all four corners simultaneously with pre-planned moves. Since all edge clocks are solved, the corners can be corrected with specific pin-and-wheel combinations that affect only corner positions.
tips: Learn the corner-only move sequences
Each corner move should be executed quickly and precisely
The simultaneous nature of this step saves significant time
Practice corner isolation until it becomes automatic

pros: Good balance between speed and learning difficulty
Clear progression path from beginner to advanced Clock solving
cons: Not fast enough for world-record-level solves
